A Pair of Firsts Before Breakfast

Tuesday, May 26, 2026 · 5 species· 27 detections ★ 2 life-list first

The black-headed grosbeak is a medium-sized, seed-eating bird in the family Cardinalidae. It is sometimes considered conspecific with the rose-breasted grosbeak with which it hybridizes on the American Great Plains.

Today's jaunt rewarded me with not one but two additions to my life list, both with that jolt of excitement only a "first" can deliver. While easing along a familiar path, the fluting song of a Black-headed Grosbeak cut through the morning—a bright, clear melody that stopped me in my tracks. There's an unmistakable satisfaction in finally hearing a bird you've read about for so long and realizing, yes, that's a grosbeak.

Not long after, the nasal calls of a Steller’s Jay bounced through the trees—sharp, a bit brash, exactly as the field guides promised. Sharing space with a California Scrub-Jay (an old friend by now) made for a lively jay debate in the canopy.

The background soundtrack was full of energy, too: Olive-sided Flycatchers announcing themselves from high perches and, tucked into the shrubbery, the MacGillivray's Warbler with its secretive song. Add in good local eBird activity lately, and it feels like migration is keeping the woods bustling.

Days like this are why I get up early—not for the numbers, but for the thrill of discovery, unexpected or long-awaited.

Today's cast

MacGillivray's Warbler
Geothlypis tolmiei
×10 · 98% best confidence · 06:07–06:20 ✓ local

MacGillivray's warbler is a species of New World warbler. These birds are sluggish and heavy warblers, preferring to spend most of their time on, or near the ground, except when singing.

Olive-sided Flycatcher
Contopus cooperi
×9 · 92% best confidence · 06:09–06:19 ✓ local

The olive-sided flycatcher is a small to medium sized passerine bird in the family Tyrannidae, the tyrant flycatcher family.

California Scrub-Jay
Aphelocoma californica
×4 · 84% best confidence · 06:09–06:19 ✓ local

The California scrub jay is a species of scrub jay native to western North America. It ranges from southern British Columbia throughout California and western Nevada near Reno to west of the Sierra Nevada.

Black-headed Grosbeak
Pheucticus melanocephalus
×3 · 88% best confidence · 06:07–06:15 ★ first ✓ local

The black-headed grosbeak is a medium-sized, seed-eating bird in the family Cardinalidae. It is sometimes considered conspecific with the rose-breasted grosbeak with which it hybridizes on the American Great Plains.

Steller's Jay
Cyanocitta stelleri
×1 · 84% best confidence · 06:17 ★ first ✓ local

Steller's jay is a bird native to western North America and the mountains of Central America, closely related to the blue jay found in eastern North America. It is the only crested jay west of the Rocky Mountains.
